text:
Once upon a time, in a forest where the trees were as old as time itself, lived a humble frog named Frodo. Frodo was not very big or flashy—his green skin couldn’t bend light, and he never traveled far from his cozy home by the water hole. But Frodo had friends, many friends.

One day, a strange cloud passed overhead and dropped a shiny stone by Frodo’s pool. Curious, he picked it up. As he looked at it, it started talking. It said it was magical, infused with the ancient magic of stars.

Frodo hid the stone. Night after night, it would talk, showing him visions of distant lands and galaxies. It called itself Galileo, and it urged him to leave his home and see the world.

After much deliberation and a few bouts with doubting himself, Frodo decided to go. He packed a small bundle with essentials and set out on his adventure. Weeks turned into months. Frodo
